summary: The reactive scheduling pipeline already exists and works: tasks/workflows/settings flow from useTaskStore through storeConnector into useSchedulerStore.setInputs, which runs the ONE UnifiedScheduler engine and publishes scheduleResult/scheduledItems/nextScheduledItem that GanttChart, StartNextTaskWidget, WeeklyCalendar, and the chat context all read reactively. A recomputeSchedule() store action even exists already — but no UI element anywhere exposes it (it is only called programmatically when sprint mode is toggled). The schedule therefore goes stale in two ways that only a full page refresh currently fixes: (1) time-anchoring — the engine schedules everything from the getCurrentTime() captured at the last input change, and nothing ever recomputes as wall-clock time advances; and (2) external data — task changes made outside the renderer (AI agent server-side, Vision Pro client, other sessions) never reach useTaskStore because the only full refetch is initializeData() on app mount/session switch. The requested button is a thin feature: wire a refresh action (refetch + unconditional recomputeSchedule) to a button in the Gantt toolbar / work status widget; every consumer already updates reactively via Zustand selectors, so no other changes are needed.
rootCause: Two confirmed staleness mechanisms, plus the missing UI affordance. (A) No time-driven recompute: useSchedulerStore.computeSchedule captures getCurrentTime() once per recompute (src/renderer/store/useSchedulerStore.ts:164) and passes it as context.currentTime/config.startDate (:178-194); the engine anchors all task starts to MAX(blockStart, currentTime) (src/shared/unified-scheduler.ts:427-429, :696-698). Recomputes are triggered ONLY by content changes: storeConnector.ts:73-169 (tasks/workflows/settings, gated by comparison keys), :172-194 (workPatterns), SchedulingSettings.tsx:83-89 (prefs), and useTaskStore.ts:588-592 (sprint toggle → the sole recomputeSchedule() caller). GanttChart's per-minute timer (GanttChart.tsx:160-165) only moves the now-line display, and useWorkPatternStore's per-minute refreshDerivedState (useWorkPatternStore.ts:238-241) doesn't mutate the workPatterns array, so neither triggers recompute. As time passes with no edits, scheduleResult stays anchored to a stale "now". (B) No refetch path: the renderer's task data is only loaded by initializeData() at app mount (App.tsx:~264) and session change (App.tsx:~986); writes from the AI agent / other clients never reach useTaskStore, so the connector never fires. A page refresh re-runs both (refetch + fresh-time recompute), which is exactly what the user is manually substituting for. (C) The store action recomputeSchedule() (useSchedulerStore.ts:457-480) already re-runs the engine with fresh getCurrentTime() over the last-pushed inputs, but no button/menu item anywhere calls it — confirmed by repo-wide grep (only useTaskStore.ts:590 and tests).

files.detail: The reactive scheduler store. computeSchedule (:151-234) captures getCurrentTime() at :164; setInputs (:344-432) recomputes only when tasks/sequencedTasks/workPatterns/workSettings/schedulingPreferences change; recomputeSchedule (:457-480) already exists, re-runs engine + re-derives scheduledItems/nextScheduledItem, but has no UI caller; clearSchedule (:482-495) used on session switch.

The only automatic trigger wiring: useTaskStore subscription (:73-169, debounced, comparison-key gated, sprint/completed filtering at :101-125) and workPattern subscription (:172-194) push into setInputs. No time-based trigger exists.

Sole recomputeSchedule() caller at :590 (setSprintModeEnabled). initializeData (:274-340) is the full refetch (tasks+workflows via getDatabase()), run only at app mount/session change. startNextTask reads useSchedulerStore.getState().nextScheduledItem at :1697 (fresh at call time).

Primary consumer: scheduledItems (:141) and scheduleResult (:142) via reactive selectors; debugInfo/metrics derived in an effect (:513-520) and passed to ScheduleMetricsPanel/SchedulingDebugInfo; per-minute timer (:160-165) only updates the now-line. Freeze/Unfreeze snapshot toolbar (:190-222) is the natural home for the new Re-run button. No refresh button exists today.

Start-next-task widget: reads scheduleResult (:46) + nextTaskSkipIndex (:47) and computes the next task locally in a useMemo (:92-171) that re-runs instantly on store change — fully reactive, needs zero changes. Rendered inside WorkStatusWidget.tsx:154.
Consumer: scheduledItems via useSchedulerStore() (:21), grouped per day (:27-39). Fully reactive.
Consumer: one-shot useSchedulerStore.getState() reads (:90, :120, :129) — always fresh at next chat message, no change needed.

The ONE engine. scheduleForDisplay (:382) consumes context.currentTime; task starts clamp to MAX(blockStart, currentTime) (:427-429) and day-0 scheduling respects config.currentTime (:696-698) — so re-running with a fresh now produces a re-anchored schedule.
Change gates for the connector: createTaskComparisonKey (:15-35) and createSequencedTaskComparisonKey (:40-67). Note: task key omits Task.dependencies (declared at src/shared/types.ts:29).
Per-minute refreshDerivedState interval (:238-241) updates currentBlock/nextBlock only; does not mutate workPatterns, so it never triggers a scheduler recompute.

proposedFix: 1) Add a refreshSchedule() async action to useTaskStore (beside setSprintModeEnabled, src/renderer/store/useTaskStore.ts ~:585) that (a) refetches tasks + sequencedTasks via getDatabase().getTasks()/getSequencedTasks() and sets them with new array references (same pattern as initializeData :330, but WITHOUT clearing stores or resetting nextTaskSkipIndex, so the UI never flashes empty), (b) calls useWorkPatternStore's existing load action to refetch patterns, and (c) after the connector's debounce window — or simply unconditionally — calls useSchedulerStore.getState().recomputeSchedule() so the schedule re-anchors to the current getCurrentTime() even when no content changed (the connector won't fire if comparison keys are identical, which is exactly the time-staleness case). This is safe because recomputeSchedule already exists, is idempotent, and only rewrites scheduleResult/scheduledItems/nextScheduledItem in the scheduler store. 2) Add the button: an IconRefresh/IconSync Button in GanttChart's Card toolbar next to the Freeze/Unfreeze snapshot controls (GanttChart.tsx ~:850, 'Scheduled Tasks (Priority Order)' card) and a mini variant in the StartNextTaskWidget header row (StartNextTaskWidget.tsx :263-270, beside the skip button), both invoking useTaskStore(s => s.refreshSchedule) with a loading state. No business logic in components — the button only dispatches the store action (CLAUDE.md rule). 3) No consumer changes needed: GanttChart (scheduledItems/scheduleResult selectors :141-142 + debugInfo effect :513-520 feeding ScheduleMetricsPanel/SchedulingDebugInfo), StartNextTaskWidget (useMemo over scheduleResult :92-171), WeeklyCalendar (:21), chat-context-provider and useTaskStore.startNextTask (:1697, getState at call time) all read the scheduler store reactively or at call time, so one recompute repopulates everything without a page refresh.
testPlan: Vitest (existing framework; mcp__diagnostic__run_tests). (1) Store-level regression in src/renderer/store/__tests__/useSchedulerStore.refresh.test.ts: seed useSchedulerStore via setInputs with a task set and work patterns using the mock time-provider; advance the mocked time (setCurrentTime) past the first scheduled item's start; assert scheduleResult is unchanged (proves the staleness bug); call recomputeSchedule() and assert scheduled item startTimes re-anchor to the new now and nextScheduledItem updates. (2) Action-level test in src/renderer/store/useTaskStore.scheduling.test.ts (file already exists, mirrors this pattern): mock getDatabase() to return changed tasks, call refreshSchedule(), assert tasks are refetched, nextTaskSkipIndex is preserved, and useSchedulerStore.recomputeSchedule was invoked (spy). (3) Component smoke test alongside GanttChart.test.tsx: render the Gantt toolbar, click the new refresh button, assert the store action fires once and a loading state toggles.
adjacentFindings: createTaskComparisonKey (src/shared/utils/store-comparison.ts:15-35) omits Task.dependencies (src/shared/types.ts:29), so editing ONLY a standalone task's dependencies never triggers the storeConnector to recompute the schedule — the Gantt keeps the old ordering until some other field changes or the app reloads.

No time-driven recompute exists anywhere: even without the button, the schedule silently drifts as wall-clock time passes (work blocks end, items' startTimes fall into the past). A low-frequency timer (e.g. the existing per-minute useWorkPatternStore interval at useWorkPatternStore.ts:238-241) could also call recomputeSchedule() when the current block boundary changes — worth considering as a follow-up beyond the manual button.

recomputeSchedule() (useSchedulerStore.ts:457-480) reuses the connector's last-pushed inputs but, unlike setInputs (:386-387), does not re-filter corrupted entries (tasks without ids); harmless today since inputs were pre-filtered when pushed, but the two paths could drift.

Stale-data double-checking is duplicated: StartNextTaskWidget re-implements the work-item filtering/skip logic locally (:92-171) in parallel with the store's extractNextScheduledItem (useSchedulerStore.ts:236-328), explicitly to defend against stale scheduleResult (comment at :121-122). Once a reliable refresh exists, this duplicate logic is a candidate for consolidation per the 'no duplicate logic' rule.

App.tsx WorkScheduleModal onSave (:963-966) is a no-op ('Refresh any data if needed' comment) — work-schedule edits rely solely on the work-pattern store's own state updates to propagate; if the modal writes via a path that bypasses useWorkPatternStore.workPatterns, the scheduler would not recompute.
